response:
Sec. 1  
people allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their bodies react to peanut protein which is not present in water  
allergies to peanut protein are triggered by the immune system which responds to the presence of peanut protein in the environment  
the immune system in allergic individuals produces antibodies that attack peanut protein causing symptoms such as hives and swelling  
the presence of peanut protein in water does not trigger the immune system in allergic individuals  
allergic individuals cannot live underwater because their bodies are not adapted to the presence of peanut protein in water
